what_it_does: Loads the declared model capability catalog from ops/model_capabilities.json and exposes model-id-keyed queries (capabilities_for, provides_for, role_can_bind, suitable_models) that join intrinsic capabilities to the local service registry and GPU/VRAM layer; it also encodes cognition placement policy and provides a gated actuator (ensure_resident, ensure_loadout_for_mode) to load, evict, and verify residency on demand.
observations: MODEL_CAPABILITIES is populated at import by _load_catalog reading ops/model_capabilities.json; missing, malformed, or empty files raise RuntimeError.
The catalog strips file-level and row-level _-prefixed annotation keys before use.
Capability fields carry provenance (declared/probed/measured/served) and a controlled tag vocabulary (CAPABILITY_TAGS).
capabilities_for joins a catalog row to services.json via service_key_for and adds served_by, context_ceiling, vram_budget_mb, resident_capable, is_resident, and optionally a live tool probe.
provides_for returns the tag list; role_can_bind checks requires ⊆ provides; suitable_models lists all matching model-ids.
require_resident surfaces a structured fail-loud result; ensure_resident can load and evict largest-first if authorized, returning a swap-approval ask when eviction is needed but not pre-authorized.
ensure_resident reuses gpu.check_fit, gpu.plan_eviction, gpu.teardown, systemd.control, gpu.ram_fit, and _wait_until_serving instead of duplicating resource logic.
ensure_loadout_for_mode resolves the brain service from the registry brain group and loudly notes that the gpu_util variant is not applied.
standouts: The module is both a read-only capability catalog and a state-changing model-loading actuator.
ensure_resident returns a structured swap-approval ask rather than auto-evicting or silently failing.
All capability truth is declared in an external JSON file loaded fail-loud at import.
conventions: External JSON file is treated as the single source of truth (registry-is-truth).
Fail-loud behavior: raises RuntimeError/EnsureResidentError for missing catalog, unknown model, unfit load, failed start, or timeout.
Reuses existing gpu.py and systemd.py primitives rather than duplicating fit/start/evict logic.
Lazy import of runtime.suite inside ensure_loadout_for_mode to keep the stdlib-only CLI path clean.
Docstrings reference external lane identifiers (C8.1, G14, #50) and downstream files.
concerns: Import-time I/O and parsing can crash the module if the catalog file is missing or malformed.
capabilities_for performs a live HTTP probe when live_probe=True, which can block and mutate the tools field.
Magic constants: 2-second post-eviction sleep and 3-second polling interval in _wait_until_serving.
ensure_loadout_for_mode constructs Suite via __new__ to access mode_registry, bypassing normal initialization.
Heavy reliance on undocumented external functions (gpu.*, systemd.*, registry.*) makes the file hard to understand in isolation.
notes: A model capability registry that reads an external JSON catalog, joins it to local service/GPU state, and doubles as a gated, fail-loud model-loading actuator with a swap-approval ask.
questions: What is the exact schema of ops/model_capabilities.json and how is it kept in sync with services.json?
What are the contracts of gpu.budget_vram, gpu.check_fit, gpu.plan_eviction, gpu.teardown, gpu.ram_fit, and systemd.control?
Who is the intended caller of ensure_resident and ensure_loadout_for_mode (CLI, cognition engine, MCP tool)?
How does suite.py consume provides_for to replace its hand-derived capability_providers?
What happens when an unknown model-id is passed to capabilities_for versus role_can_bind?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring and functions describe an intrinsic capability registry joined to service/GPU layers plus a gated residency actuator, and the code implements exactly those capabilities.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing resource primitives; no duplicate GPU/systemd logic.
Separate read-only catalog queries from state-changing actuators.
Cloud/resident placement policy encoded as data.
intention_signals: To make model capability facts file-discovered and authoritative, prevent incorrect role-to-model binding, and give operators/agents a single explicit, approval-gated path to load or swap models on the local GPU.
novelty: It merges a static capability catalog with an active resource actuator, and uses a structured swap-approval return value to avoid both silent auto-eviction and hard failure when loading needs room.
contribution: A model-id-keyed capability registry with external JSON catalog, provenance-tagged fields, role-binding queries, and a unified gated model-residency actuator that reuses existing GPU/systemd primitives.
summary_for_embedding: company/ops/cli/capabilities.py loads an external model capability catalog and exposes model-id-keyed queries joined to local service and GPU state, plus a gated ensure_resident actuator for loading or swapping models on demand.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to centralize model capability truth in a declared registry and provide a fail-loud, approval-gated path for making models resident.

symbols.does: Loads and validates the external model capability JSON catalog.
symbols.description: _load_catalog reads ops/model_capabilities.json, rejects missing, malformed, or empty files with RuntimeError, and returns a dict of model rows with annotation keys stripped.

symbols.does: Re-reads the catalog into the live MODEL_CAPABILITIES dict.
symbols.description: reload_catalog calls _load_catalog and updates the global MODEL_CAPABILITIES in place, used to prove add-a-row behavior without code edits.

symbols.does: Finds the local service-key whose config.model matches a model-id.
symbols.description: service_key_for scans the services.json registry for a service whose config.model equals the given model-id, returning the key or None for cloud/unbacked models.

symbols.does: Builds the local OpenAI-compatible endpoint URL for a service-key.
symbols.description: _endpoint_for reads the service config or service-level port and returns http://127.0.0.1:{port}/v1, or None if no port is declared.

symbols.does: Live-probes whether a served endpoint supports tool calling.
symbols.description: _probe_tools sends a forced-tool-choice chat completion to the endpoint and returns True if tool_calls are present, False if absent, or None on failure.

symbols.does: Returns the full capability record for a model-id joined to service/GPU state.
symbols.description: capabilities_for copies the catalog row, adds served_by and service-derived context_ceiling, vram_budget_mb, resident_capable, is_resident, and endpoint, and optionally upgrades the tools provenance via a live probe; unknown ids return a fail-loud ASK dict.

symbols.does: Returns the entire joined capability catalog.
symbols.description: catalog loads the registry and returns a dict mapping every known model-id to its capabilities_for result.

symbols.does: Returns the capability tag set provided by a model-id.
symbols.description: provides_for looks up a model-id in MODEL_CAPABILITIES and returns its provides list, or an empty list for unknown models.

symbols.does: Checks whether a role's required tags are satisfied by a model-id.
symbols.description: role_can_bind returns True iff the requires set is a subset of the model's provides set; unknown models yield False.

symbols.does: Lists all model-ids whose provided tags satisfy a requirement set.
symbols.description: suitable_models iterates MODEL_CAPABILITIES and returns every model-id for which role_can_bind is True.

symbols.does: Returns the cognition placement policy for a named track.
symbols.description: placement_for indexes COGNITION_PLACEMENT_POLICY by track and raises KeyError for unknown tracks.

symbols.does: Asserts the swarm remains resident regardless of main-brain choice.
symbols.description: swarm_survives_cloud_brain returns True exactly when the swarm track is declared resident-always.

symbols.does: Reports whether a model-id is currently loaded and active.
symbols.description: is_resident joins the model-id to its service-key and checks whether that key appears in gpu.running_gpu_services.

symbols.does: Fail-loudly asserts that a model-id is resident.
symbols.description: require_resident returns a structured result: success if resident, an ASK if no local service exists, or an OFFER_LOAD with the company up command if the service exists but is not active.

symbols.does: Exception raised when a model cannot be made resident.
symbols.description: EnsureResidentError is a RuntimeError subclass used by ensure_resident for no service, unfit load, failed start, or timeout.

symbols.does: Disambiguates an argument to a local service-key.
symbols.description: _resolve_service_key returns the argument if it is already a services.json key, otherwise resolves it as a model-id via service_key_for.

symbols.does: Blocks until a service's declared port is accepting connections.
symbols.description: _wait_until_serving polls systemd.port_open for up to timeout seconds and returns False if the unit fails or the port never opens.

symbols.does: Gated actuator that loads a service/model and evicts if authorized.
symbols.description: ensure_resident checks fit, optionally executes gpu.plan_eviction, verifies system RAM, starts the service via systemd.control, waits for the port, and returns a structured result; it returns a swap-approval ask when eviction is needed but not authorized, and raises EnsureResidentError on impossible or failed loads.

symbols.does: Ensures the brain service for a named mode is resident.
symbols.description: ensure_loadout_for_mode reads a mode's brain_config from the mode registry, resolves the brain service by group, calls ensure_resident, and loudly reports that the gpu_util variant is not applied.
